2606.27334.title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
2606.27334.paper_summary: Digital twins have emerged as a promising paradigm for personalized healthcare, enabling modeling of individual behavior and health trajectories. In cognitive health, early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) remains challenging, where language and conversational patterns serve as non-invasive biomarkers. In this work, we propose a language-based digital twin framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) to mimic the conversational behavior of elderly individuals by incorporating stylometric cues and contextual metadata. To evaluate fidelity and cognitive consistency, we introduce a multi-head conditional variational autoencoder (cVAE) that jointly measures reconstruction quality and predicts cognitive scores. Experiments on the I-CONECT dataset show that the digital twin preserves identity-specific characteristics and achieves reconstruction and MoCA prediction errors comparable to real data, while outperforming baseline GPT-generated responses. These results highlight the potential of language-based digital twins as a scalable and non-invasive approach for personalized and continuous cognitive health monitoring.
2606.27334.paper_summary_zh: 數位雙胞胎已成為個人化醫療的一個有前景的範式，使得個體行為和健康軌跡的建模成為可能。在認知健康方面，輕度認知障礙（MCI）的早期檢測仍然具有挑戰性，其中語言和對話模式作為非侵入性生物標誌。本文提出了一個基於語言的數位雙胞胎框架，利用大型語言模型（LLMs）模擬老年人的對話行為，並結合風格特徵和上下文元數據。為了評估忠實度和認知一致性，我們引入了一個多頭條件變分自編碼器（cVAE），該模型共同測量重建質量並預測認知分數。在I-CONECT數據集上的實驗顯示，數位雙胞胎保留了身份特定的特徵，並且在重建和MoCA預測誤差方面與真實數據相當，同時超越了基準GPT生成的回應。這些結果突顯了基於語言的數位雙胞胎作為一種可擴展且非侵入性的方法，用於個性化和持續的認知健康監測的潛力。
2606.27334.author: Mohammad Mehdi Hosseini et.al.
2606.27334.authors: Mohammad Mehdi Hosseini, Mohammad H. Mahoor, Hiroko H. Dodge
